subject: chemistry
title: 22M.1.SL.TZ2.20
question_html:
What is the strongest acid in the equation below?

H3AsO4 + H2O ⇌ H2AsO4− + H3O+      Kc = 4.5 × 10−4

A.  H3AsO4

B.  H2O

C.  H2AsO4−

D.  H3O+
examiners_html: A challenging question that combined interpreting the implications of the value of the equilibrium constant with the Brønsted-Lowry concept. Most candidates focused on BL theory and the forward reaction only. 36% of the candidates answered the question correctly by selecting the acid involved in the backward reaction.
